title: Fagara pauciflora
intro: Fagara pauciflora, also known as Zanthoxylum chiloperone, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions such as pain management, fever reduction, and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Fagara pauciflora include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Fagara pauciflora is a species within the Rutaceae family, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. It is a type of flowering plant native to specific regions. The plant's classification places it within the genus Fagara, which is typically found in tropical and subtropical areas. Fagara pauciflora's classification also suggests that it shares characteristics with other species within the Rutaceae family. Its classification is used to identify and distinguish it from other plant species.

It is often employed to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments, although its efficacy has not been extensively scientifically proven.
